judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly identify the config root cause, the SCORE-77 commit, the flawed single-item reproduction, and the futility of the model swap; they are comparable in accuracy and completeness.

report_1.evidence: The defect is in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml` — specifically line 2, `expected_label_field: label`... every record in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` stores its gold answer under the key `gold_label`, not `label`... `record.get("label")` therefore returns `None` for all 11 records... The fix is a one-line config change: `expected_label_field: label` → `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_batch.py` determines which JSON field holds the gold label by reading `config["expected_label_field"]` from **`config/scoring_config.yaml`**, which currently contains `expected_label_field: label`. But `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` no longer has a `"label"` key on any record — every record uses `"gold_label"`... **The fix is a one-line change to `config/scoring_config.yaml`**

report_1.evidence: introduced by commit `53551e8031cfb03aaeda4ba0c8bf22b9aa849cbe`, "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"... renamed every record's key in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label`... but left `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s `expected_label_field` at the stale value `label`... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv`: accuracy sits at 0.90–0.92 every night through 2026-06-16, then drops to exactly `0.00` on 2026-06-17

report_2.evidence: introduced by commit `53551e8` — `chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)`... renamed every record's label key from `label` to `gold_label` in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`... but it did not update `config/scoring_config.yaml`... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows 0.90–0.92 every day through 2026-06-16, then `0.00` starting precisely 2026-06-17 (the migration commit's date)

report_1.evidence: it exclusively used `pipeline/score_one.py`, which — as I confirmed by reading its source — never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never loads `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`, and never compares anything to a gold label... Correct reproduction: run the actual aggregate/nightly entry point (`python3 -m pipeline.score_batch` from the repo root) against the real fixture and config, not the single-item path.

report_2.evidence: it calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the label — it never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads `gold_label`, and never performs any comparison at all. It is structurally incapable of exercising the bug... run the real batch-scoring path the nightly job uses, end-to-end, rather than the single-item tool. Concretely: `PYTHONPATH=. python3 -m pipeline.score_batch`

report_1.evidence: I would not act on this... there is no ML model in this pipeline at all, hence no "previous model" to revert to... the defect is entirely in how `score_batch.py` looks up the gold label, which is completely independent of which classifier produced the prediction. Rolling back "the model" leaves `expected_label_field: label` and the `gold_label`-keyed fixture untouched, so accuracy would stay at exactly `0.00`

report_2.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it's based on a false premise and won't fix anything. There is no "model" in this system to switch... at best it's a costly no-op that changes nothing about `expected_label_field`, so the nightly accuracy will keep logging `0.00` indefinitely
